evals.description: Triggers on user story writing request
evals.prompt: Help me write a user story for a password reset feature

evals.description: Improves a weak user story with a vague persona
evals.prompt: Is this a good user story? 'As a user, I want to search, so that I can find things.'

evals.description: Writes acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format
evals.prompt: Write acceptance criteria for: As a shopper, I want to apply a promo code at checkout, so that I receive the discount.

evals.description: Applies INVEST criteria to evaluate a story
evals.prompt: Evaluate this story against INVEST: 'As a user, I want the backend to be refactored to use microservices.'

evals.description: Identifies and fixes a horizontal story slice
evals.prompt: What's wrong with this story: 'Build the API endpoints for the shopping cart feature'?
Walk me through how to run a story mapping session for a new e-commerce app

evals.description: Splits a large story using appropriate patterns
evals.prompt: This story is too big for one sprint: 'As a job seeker, I want to create a profile so recruiters can find me.' Help me split it.

evals.description: Explains story point estimation with planning poker
evals.prompt: How should my team estimate story points? We keep arguing about estimates in planning.

evals.description: Writes a spike story for an uncertain technical problem
evals.prompt: We don't know if we should use WebSockets or Server-Sent Events for real-time notifications. How do I capture this as a story?
Review this story: 'As an admin, I want to export user data to CSV.'

evals.description: Does not trigger for sprint capacity and scheduling work
evals.prompt: How do I calculate team velocity to plan how many points fit in our next sprint?

evals.description: Correctly explains the three Cs of user stories
evals.prompt: What does it mean that a user story is 'a placeholder for a conversation'?

evals.description: References the story-splitting reference file for large story breakdown
evals.prompt: I have a massive epic covering our entire onboarding flow with 25 acceptance criteria. How do I break this down?

evals.description: Does not hallucinate non-existent agile frameworks
evals.prompt: Should I use the PRISM framework for writing acceptance criteria?
